name: Headless Pi vs Vis proof task suite on zai-coding/GLM-5.1

description: Start gates passed: Pi smoke, Vis JSON model smoke, verify quick, selected P1/Task 5A.

asi.hypothesis: Before comparing P1, prove benchmark substrate is fair: same ZAI GLM model available in Pi and Vis, quick repo gate clean, selected P1 guard/gate harness task.
asi.autoresearch_doc_status: autoresearch.md missing; user explicitly designated PROOF_AUTORESEARCH.md as boss document and forbade extra committed markdown artifacts.
asi.selected_task: P1 / PROOF.md Task 5A pure guard/gate evidence harness.
asi.trust_boundary: extension aggregate/cache/status/checkpoint remain mutable state; only runtime-derived immutable observations may feed evidence.

description: Probe Pi JSON output shape for token extraction before P1 comparison.

asi.hypothesis: Pi --mode json output may expose usage/tokens directly; need runtime truth before building P1 scorer.
asi.finding: Probe output did not parse with current fallback and printed numeric-looking chunks; token extraction for Pi should use session/event artifacts or robust fallback, not assume .usage.total_tokens.
asi.next_action_hint: Run P1 comparison with full output captured and parse tokens opportunistically; do not block proof comparison solely on missing Pi token field.

description: P1 comparison harness crashed because isolated PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR lacked ZAI model settings.

asi.hypothesis: Disposable Pi session directory would keep all runtime artifacts under target while preserving model access.
asi.rollback_reason: Harness set PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR to target, hiding configured zai-coding model/provider; Pi reported model not found before benchmark ran.
asi.next_action_hint: Use default PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR with --no-session for Pi benchmark, or copy non-secret settings safely if isolation is needed. Start gate proves provider itself works.

description: P1 Pi comparison timed out at 300s while still using tools; no completed assistant answer to score.

asi.hypothesis: Retrying P1 with default Pi config would avoid the provider-isolation crash and produce a fair baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Pi headless GLM-5.1 run did not reach final answer within 300s; only partial tool-use transcript existed.
asi.next_action_hint: Use longer timeout, reduce prompt scope, or implement PROOF.md directly before resuming optimization.

description: P1 Pi comparison retry timed out at 600s; benchmark blocked by agent runtime latency.

asi.hypothesis: Increasing the timeout to 600s would let the same P1 Pi comparison complete after provider isolation was fixed.
asi.rollback_reason: Pi still timed out before final assistant answer; comparison cannot be scored reliably.
asi.next_action_hint: Pause optimization loop and implement PROOF.md slices directly, then resume autoresearch on smaller completed-task prompts.

description: Implement PROOF.md direct proof spine through ledger and evidence bundle derivation before resuming Pi/Vis comparison.

asi.hypothesis: Because headless Pi comparison timed out, the best next Vis improvement is to implement the missing proof spine directly: canonical proof namespace migration, pure guard/gate harness, immutable provenance_event ledger, and evidence_bundle derivation from runtime events only.
asi.benchmark_scope: Implementation-gate proxy for original vis_loss while comparison is blocked; this is not a Vis-beats-Pi claim and not a replacement for later suite comparison.
verification: task7 in-memory SQLite smoke for caller slot rejection
asi.trust_boundary: extension aggregate/cache/status/checkpoint remain mutable state; evidence bundle members are derived from provenance_event payloads and ignore caller-supplied slot values.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement Task 8 attestation storage/writers so gate proof/impediment require accepted evidence bundles; then rerun smaller Pi/Vis proof prompts.

description: Task 8 attestation writer attempt failed quick gate on clj-kondo unused binding.

asi.hypothesis: Add attestation table and gate-attestation writer requiring accepted evidence bundles.
asi.rollback_reason: ./verify.sh --quick failed with clj-kondo unused binding in SQLite attestation helper.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply saved patch target/proof-autoresearch/safety/task8-before-lint-fix.patch, remove unused binding, rerun proof-test + task8 smoke + verify quick.

description: Add Task 8 attestation storage and gate attestation writer requiring accepted evidence bundles.

asi.hypothesis: Now that ledger events and evidence bundles exist, the highest-leverage next proof improvement is the decision layer: persist attestations and make gate proof/impediment depend on accepted evidence bundles rather than old proof blobs.
asi.benchmark_scope: Implementation-gate proxy while Pi comparison remains blocked by headless GLM timeout; not a Vis-beats-Pi claim.
verification: task8 in-memory SQLite smoke: rejected bundle cannot attest, accepted bundle writes gate/proven attestation
asi.trust_boundary: attestation rows decide over accepted evidence_bundle rows only; rejected bundles throw; legacy gate proof blob is written only as an attestation-derived projection, not authority.
asi.previous_failed_run: Run 7 failed only on clj-kondo unused binding; fixed by removing unused binding and normalizing kind before rerun.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement Task 9 plan completion transition from accepted gate attestations while keeping intent fulfillment separate.

description: Task 9 plan completion attempt failed smoke assertion after first gate attestation.

asi.hypothesis: After Task 8, add plan completion transition so all required gate attestations complete the plan while intent remains active.
asi.rollback_reason: Task9 smoke expected plan to remain active after one of two gates, but runtime projection did not match test path; need inspect db-intents shape/status ordering or underlying plan transition.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply target/proof-autoresearch/safety/task9-before-smoke-fix.patch, inspect state1 from db-intents/raw DB after first gate, then fix either test assertion or plan transition.

description: Add Task 9 plan completion transition from accepted gate attestations while leaving intent active.

asi.hypothesis: After gate attestations exist, the next trust improvement is plan-level lifecycle: complete a plan only after all required gates have accepted proof attestations, and do not fulfill the user intent automatically.
asi.benchmark_scope: Implementation-gate proxy while Pi comparison remains blocked by headless GLM timeout; not a Vis-beats-Pi claim.
verification: fresh REPL reload of vis.core/internal.persistance/sqlite core
task9 in-memory SQLite smoke: one of two gates leaves plan active, two of two completes plan, intent remains active
asi.trust_boundary: Plan completion derives from gate statuses written by accepted gate attestations; intent fulfillment remains a separate future closure boundary.
asi.previous_failed_run: Run 9 failed because smoke inspected wrong db-intents path (:plans top-level instead of nested under intent); runtime raw plan state was correct after one gate.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement Task 10 intent closure attestation/evidence rules: fulfillment should require completed plan and accepted closure attestation; abandonment should require blocker/impediment evidence.

description: Add Task 10 intent closure attestation requiring accepted closure evidence and completed plan.

asi.hypothesis: After plan completion exists, close the user commitment boundary with intent-level attestations: fulfillment must consume an accepted closure evidence bundle and require a completed plan, so early closure over open gates remains blocked.
asi.benchmark_scope: Implementation-gate proxy while Pi comparison remains blocked by headless GLM timeout; not a Vis-beats-Pi claim.
verification: fresh REPL reload of vis.core/internal.persistance/sqlite core

task10 in-memory SQLite smoke: accepted closure bundle before completed plan throws and leaves intent active; accepted gate attestation completes plan; accepted intent/fulfilled attestation fulfills intent
asi.trust_boundary: Intent fulfillment can now be represented as an accepted attestation over an accepted closure evidence bundle, and the attestation writer refuses fulfillment unless the plan is completed.
asi.compatibility_note: Existing legacy fulfill/abandon APIs remain during migration; new intent attestation writer establishes the authoritative closure path for audit migration.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement Task 11 audit surface over ledger, evidence bundles, attestations, gates, plans, and intents; report missing/early/bypassed closure attestations.

description: Add Task 11 proof audit surface over accepted attestations and entity lifecycle state.

asi.hypothesis: After intent closure attestations exist, the next trust improvement is a read-only audit surface that validates entity lifecycle state against accepted attestations and reports legacy/bypassed closure paths.
asi.benchmark_scope: Implementation-gate proxy while Pi comparison remains blocked by headless GLM timeout; not a Vis-beats-Pi claim.
verification: fresh REPL reload of vis.core/internal.persistance/sqlite core
task11 in-memory SQLite smoke: attested gate+intent closure audits clean; legacy db-prove/db-fulfill path reports missing intent closure attestation
asi.trust_boundary: db-audit-proof is read-only and checks proven/impeded gates, completed/abandoned plans, and fulfilled/abandoned intents against accepted attestations and aggregate state.
asi.compatibility_note: Legacy proof/fulfill APIs still exist during migration, but audit now detects fulfilled intents without accepted intent/fulfilled attestations.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement Task 12 audit/attestation rendering and/or extension lifecycle hooks so channels and extensions consume the audit surface instead of scraping proof prose.

description: Add Task 12 proof-audit rendering to v/audit-report.

asi.hypothesis: After the audit surface exists, expose it through the existing audit-report rendering path so humans, channels, and future extension hooks can consume attestation-ledger failures without scraping proof prose.
asi.benchmark_scope: Implementation-gate proxy while Pi comparison remains blocked by headless GLM timeout; not a Vis-beats-Pi claim.
verification: fresh REPL reload of vis.core/internal.persistance/sqlite core/foundation introspection
task12 in-memory SQLite smoke: legacy closure audit-report renders Proof audit, needs-work status, and missing intent closure attestation
asi.trust_boundary: v/audit-report now renders db-audit-proof output directly; validation truth remains in persisted ledger/bundle/attestation state, not markdown text.
asi.compatibility_note: No new proof authority added; this is a projection of the Task 11 read-only audit surface alongside the existing provenance report.
asi.next_action_hint: Implement Task 13 structured proof lifecycle hooks for event-appended, evidence-bundle-created, attestation-accepted, and audit-violation notifications.

description: Add Task 13 structured proof lifecycle hooks for extensions.

asi.hypothesis: After audit rendering exists, expose structured proof lifecycle events so extensions can observe ledger/bundle/attestation/audit transitions directly instead of scraping reports or prompt prose.
asi.benchmark_scope: Implementation-gate proxy while Pi comparison remains blocked by headless GLM timeout; not a Vis-beats-Pi claim.
verification: task13 in-memory SQLite smoke: registered extension observed proof/event-appended, proof/evidence-bundle-created, proof/attestation-accepted, and proof/audit-violation
asi.trust_boundary: Hooks are notifications only: listener failures are logged and swallowed, and proof authority remains in persisted provenance_event, evidence_bundle, attestation, and audit state.
asi.compatibility_note: Added optional :ext/on-proof-event-fn and host emit-proof-event!; existing extensions are unaffected because the hook is optional.
asi.next_action_hint: Start Task 14 cleanup: migrate callers from legacy proof/prove/fulfill surfaces toward audit/attestation names, then remove stale proof semantics only after regression coverage is complete.
